title: B1U3 Family matters Project
content: The presentation introduces a project on planning an itinerary for a family day out. It is part of a course titled 'Family matters' in a Shenzhen high school.

title: Lead-in
content: The slide introduces the acronym 'Family' as standing for 'Father and mother I love you.' It emphasizes the importance of family love and unity.

title: Lead-in
content: This slide asks viewers to consider the first word that comes to mind regarding family. It sets the stage for personal reflection on family values.

title: How much do you know about your family?
content: The slide explores topics like family culture, vocabulary, and understanding ideas. It includes examples like differences in family culture and changes in family appearance.

title: Draw the mind map
content: A mind map is displayed illustrating respect for family culture, knowing physical changes, and accepting points of view within family dynamics.

title: How do you deal with your family relationship?
content: Different strategies for resolving family relationships are outlined. This includes understanding ideas, listening, speaking, and developing new ideas.

title: Draw the mind map
content: This slide presents a mind map on relationship management within families, focusing on controversies, daily matters, and conflicts.

title: How deep do you love your family?
content: The slide encourages writing about family memories and planning day-outs. Emphasis is placed on recalling lovely family memories and spending quality time together.

title: Draw the mind map
content: A mind map further connects the concepts of writing about a family memory and planning a family day out, under the theme of family ties.

title: Mind map of Unit 3
content: The final mind map combines all elements into strengthening the value of family relationships, incorporating knowledge, respect, and encouragement.

title: Love Value Know
content: This slide presents a brief hierarchy emphasizing the stages of understanding, valuing, and loving family.

title: Planning an itinerary for a family day out
content: Groups are encouraged to role-play scenarios emphasizing different aspects of family dynamics. This involves organizing meetings and resolving conflicts.

title: Assess and evaluate
content: A rubric is provided for evaluating the planning of a family day out. Criteria include content, ability, and delivery, rated on a scale of 1 to 3.

title: What is the meaning of Family matters?
content: The slide explains that family matters are infused with love and argues that family and related emotions are what truly matter.

title: What is a family?
content: The slide asks viewers to consider what 'family' means, suggesting creating a word cloud from the first words that come to mind.
